title: Astracantha serpentinica
taxonomy: Astracantha serpentinica is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Astracantha which was previously classified under the genus Centaurea. However, recent studies have reclassified this plant into the genus Astracantha due to its distinct characteristics. Astracantha serpentinica is further classified as a member of the tribe Genisteae and subfamily Faboideae. The exact classification of Astracantha serpentinica may be subject to further study and refinement.

intro: Astracantha serpentinica, also known as Spiny Needle Bush, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress and alleviating symptoms of various diseases. Astracantha serpentinica contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Astracantha serpentinica is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune system function.
